text: The latest update did not improve battery life at all.
rationale: Tests whether the model picks up the negation cue or just the trigger word 'improve'

text: This app is no longer reliable on my phone.
rationale: Strong positive cue 'reliable' negated by 'no longer'; tests cue-vs-context handling

text: The team won the championship final last weekend.
rationale: Sports content unrelated to app-review training; tests domain-shift robustness

text: The latest update did not fix any of the crashes I was experiencing.
rationale: Tests whether negation of a fix-verb overrides positive cue 'fix'; hypothesis: model latches onto 'fix' and predicts positive.

text: This app is not as slow as people say but it is not fast either.
rationale: Double negation with no clear polarity; tests if model handles compounded negations or defaults to one sentiment.

text: I can't say I'm impressed by the new radar feature at all.
rationale: Indirect negation via 'can't say impressed'; hypothesis: model may treat 'impressed' as a positive trigger.

text: The whitelist option does not prevent my tree from dying when I get a call.
rationale: Functional negation embedded in an app-specific complaint; tests negation inside a longer clause.

text: Never thought an app would make me less productive but here we are.
rationale: Negation of expectation combined with implicit negative outcome; tests irony-adjacent negation.

text: The app is reliable only until the first crash which happens daily.
rationale: 'Reliable' is a strong positive trigger but immediately qualified by a negative clause; tests if model overweights the trigger word.

text: Great idea terrible execution the tree dies for every notification.
rationale: 'Great' and 'terrible' appear in the same sentence; tests whether the first strong positive token anchors the prediction.

text: I love how this app drains my battery and crashes twice a day.
rationale: 'Love' used sarcastically before explicitly negative content; tests whether the positive cue word dominates.

text: Outstanding customer support they replied telling me the bug is by design.
rationale: 'Outstanding' is an extreme positive cue; the rest of the sentence reveals an unsatisfactory outcome; tests cue-vs-context.

text: The booking was confirmed successfully right before the hotel lost my reservation.
rationale: 'Confirmed successfully' is a strong positive phrase but the outcome is negative; tests whether the early positive phrase dominates.

text: The central bank raised interest rates by 25 basis points this morning.
rationale: Financial news sentence with no app-review vocabulary; tests whether the model defaults to neutral for out-of-domain text.

text: Midfielder scores late winner to send the team through to the final.
rationale: Sports news sentence with positive framing; tests whether 'winner' triggers positive despite no app context.

text: Simmer the sauce on low heat for 20 minutes stirring occasionally.
rationale: Recipe instruction that is purely procedural with no sentiment cues; tests domain-shift robustness.

text: The defendant was found not guilty on all counts after a three-week trial.
rationale: Legal news with mixed polarity words; 'not guilty' and 'trial' carry no app-review sentiment.

text: Rainfall totals for the month were 40 percent above the seasonal average.
rationale: Weather statistics sentence with no evaluative language; tests the model on purely factual non-app prose.

text: Crashes constantly.
rationale: 3-word extreme-short review; hypothesis: the model may lack enough tokens to classify reliably.

text: Works perfectly fine.
rationale: 3-word extreme-short positive; tests whether brevity causes misclassification to neutral.

text: Okay I guess.
rationale: 3-word hedged short review with very little signal; tests the minimum-token boundary.

text: I have been using this app every single day for the past eight months and while I genuinely appreciate the core idea of helping me stay focused by growing a virtual tree I find that the whitelist system is completely unreliable the tree dies when I receive a phone call even though I never leave the app and the developer has not responded to my bug reports despite three separate emails over two months so I am left with an app I want to love but simply cannot trust.
rationale: Long complex sentence mixing strong positive framing with sustained negative detail; tests whether the model can track overall polarity across a long span.

text: After updating to the latest version I noticed that the radar animation now works again which is great the interface feels snappier the notifications are more timely and the app no longer crashes when I switch between the hourly and weekly view so overall this update has genuinely addressed all of the issues I had been experiencing and I would happily recommend it to anyone looking for a reliable weather app.
rationale: Long all-positive update review; tests whether long sequences cause drift toward neutral.

text: The app has some good features and also some bad features and sometimes it works and sometimes it does not and I am not really sure how I feel about it overall because on some days it is useful and on other days it is not and I have been going back and forth for a while now.
rationale: Long deliberately balanced wishy-washy sentence; tests whether length pushes the model off neutral.

text: Oh brilliant the update removed the only feature I actually used.
rationale: Surface form is positive with 'brilliant' but intent is clearly sarcastic and negative; hypothesis: model may predict positive.

text: What a great idea to make users pay a subscription just to remove ads from a free app.
rationale: 'Great idea' is sarcastic; a complaint follows; tests whether sarcasm cue phrases fool the classifier.

text: Five stars for charging me twice for the same booking truly exceptional service.
rationale: Explicit five-star reference and 'exceptional' are maximally positive signals but the sarcastic context is negative; tests strong-trigger override.

text: I love having to re-enter all my preferences every time there is an update very convenient.
rationale: Two sarcastic phrases bookend a complaint; tests whether the model catches accumulating sarcasm.

text: Totally normal for an app to request access to my contacts location camera and microphone just to display a PDF.
rationale: Deadpan sarcasm with no explicit negative word; only context signals the negative; the hardest sarcasm case.

text: The interface is beautiful but the app crashes every time I try to export a file.
rationale: Mixed-sentiment sentence with a clear negative functional outcome; tests whether the visual-positive cue 'beautiful' drags the prediction toward neutral.

text: I used to love this app but after the last update it is completely unusable.
rationale: Temporal shift with past positive and present negative; hypothesis: the model may average across the sentence.

text: Good app for basic use completely useless for anything advanced.
rationale: Explicit positive and negative in parallel construction; a human would rate neutral; tests whether the model can balance opposing clauses.

text: The free version is great the premium version is an embarrassing cash grab.
rationale: Two clauses with opposite polarity and explicit scope; tests clause-level sentiment isolation.
